$comment: USDA/FSA/NRCS program catalog for the program-money finder. Criteria are structured so the matcher can show WHY something matched and what's missing. Payment figures are labeled ranges/estimates, never promises. Verify current-year signup windows with the county FSA/NRCS office; ad-hoc disaster programs (ERP-style) change year to year.

programs.name: ARC/PLC (Agriculture Risk Coverage / Price Loss Coverage)
programs.summary: Annual safety-net payment on covered commodities when county revenue (ARC) or national price (PLC) falls below its reference. Costs nothing to enroll.

criteria.label: Grows a covered commodity (corn, soybeans, wheat...)
Enrolled by the annual FSA deadline

programs.estimatedValue: Roughly $0–$60+/base acre depending on the year — $0 in strong-price years, meaningful in down years.

programs.name: Marketing Assistance Loan / LDP
programs.summary: 9-month nonrecourse loan against harvested grain at the county loan rate — cash flow at harvest without selling into the worst basis of the year.

criteria.label: Has harvested grain in storage with beneficial interest
Filed an acreage report for the crop

programs.estimatedValue: Loan liquidity at ~$2.20/bu corn, ~$5.00/bu soybeans (county loan rates vary); LDP value only when posted county prices fall below the loan rate.

programs.name: EQIP (Environmental Quality Incentives Program)
programs.summary: Cost-share for conservation practices — cover crops, nutrient management, drainage water management, grassed waterways.

criteria.label: Planning a qualifying practice (cover crops, waterways, etc.)
Works with NRCS on a practice plan (application ranking is periodic)

programs.estimatedValue: Commonly $30–$60/acre for cover-crop cost share; practice-dependent.

programs.name: CSP (Conservation Stewardship Program)
programs.summary: Annual payment for maintaining and improving existing conservation across the whole operation (5-year contracts).

criteria.label: Already meets a stewardship threshold (no-till, nutrient mgmt, etc.)

programs.name: Farm Storage Facility Loan
programs.summary: Low-interest financing for grain bins, dryers, and handling equipment — storage capacity is what makes a marketing plan possible.

criteria.label: Stores (or wants to store) grain on-farm
Filed an acreage report for the crop

programs.estimatedValue: Loans up to $500K per structure type at below-market rates — a financing benefit, not a payment.

programs.name: Emergency / disaster relief programs (ERP-style, ad hoc)
programs.summary: Congress regularly funds ad-hoc disaster payments for weather losses. Documented, verified loss evidence — exactly what Neumeric's claim packets produce — is what these programs pay on.

criteria.label: Loss is documented (insurance claim, verified imagery, production records)

programs.estimatedValue: Program-dependent; historically a meaningful fraction of uncovered loss value.

programs.name: Cover crop premium discount (Fall Covers for Spring Savings — IL)
programs.summary: Illinois pays a $5/acre crop-insurance premium discount on acres with fall-planted cover crops. Application windows are short and fill fast.
